description_long: This object is a weathered stone guard stone, roughly rectangular in shape, with a prominent carved skull relief on its front face. The stone surface is rough and irregular, showing signs of age, with patches of moss or lichen near the base. The skull carving is deeply set, with hollowed eyes and a pronounced jaw, giving it a striking and somewhat ominous appearance. The sides and back of the stone are unadorned, with natural stone texture and some chipping or erosion visible. The overall coloration is a mix of gray stone with greenish tints from moss and darker weathering marks. This guard stone would serve as a protective barrier at the edge of a pathway or entrance, both as a physical obstacle and a visual marker.
description: A rough, rectangular stone guard stone with a carved skull relief on the front and weathered, mossy surfaces.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed skull carving prominently, with rough stone texture and some moss at the base.
description_view_1: The rear view reveals an unadorned, weathered stone surface with no carvings or markings.
description_view_2: The angled view highlights the depth of the skull relief and the irregular, chipped edges of the stone.
description_view_3: The side view displays the profile of the skull relief protruding from the stone and the rough, uneven surface.
attribute_location: Typically found at the edge of urban streets, historical sites, or near the entrance to driveways or courtyards, often positioned to protect corners or mark boundaries.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed upright, partially embedded in the ground for stability, with the carved face oriented toward approaching traffic or pedestrians.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion on edges and corners, likely from vehicle contact and long-term weather exposure. Discoloration and smoothing on the upper surfaces from rain and environmental wear, with moss or lichen growth near the base due to ground moisture.
attribute_historical_markings: A carved skull relief on the front face, deeply incised and weathered, with no visible inscriptions or text.
